audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Mary anointing, Lazarus witness, Jerusalem entry, Greeks seeking Jesus, grain of wheat, love/hate life, lifted up, Isaiah unbelief texts, final public appeal.

verses.restored: Jesus therefore, six days before Passover, came to Bethany, where Lazarus was, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.
They made him a dinner there, and Martha was serving; Lazarus was one of those reclining with him.
Mary therefore, taking a pound of costly ointment of pure nard, anointed the feet of Jesus and wiped his feet with her hair. The house was filled with the fragrance of the ointment.
Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples, the one about to hand him over, says,
Why was this ointment not sold for three hundred denarii and given to poor people?
He said this not because it mattered to him about the poor, but because he was a thief; having the money box, he used to carry what was put into it.
Jesus therefore said, Leave her, so that she may keep it for the day of my burial.
For poor people you always have with you, but me you do not always have.
A great crowd of the Judeans therefore knew that he was there, and they came not because of Jesus only, but also to see Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
The chief priests took counsel that they might also kill Lazarus,
because on account of him many of the Judeans were going away and trusting into Jesus.
On the next day, the great crowd that had come to the feast, hearing that Jesus was coming into Jerusalem,
took palm branches and went out to meet him, and were crying out, Hosanna. Blessed is the one coming in the name of the Lord, even the King of Israel.
Jesus, finding a young donkey, sat on it, just as it has been written,
Do not fear, daughter of Zion; look, your king comes, sitting on a donkey's colt.
His disciples did not know these things at first, but when Jesus was glorified, then they remembered that these things had been written about him and that they had done these things to him.
The crowd therefore that was with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead was bearing witness.
Because of this also the crowd met him, because they heard he had done this sign.
The Pharisees therefore said to themselves, You see that you accomplish nothing; look, the world has gone after him.
Now there were some Greeks among those going up to worship at the feast.
These therefore came to Philip, the one from Bethsaida of Galilee, and were asking him, saying, Lord, we wish to see Jesus.
Philip comes and tells Andrew; Andrew and Philip come and tell Jesus.
Jesus answers them, saying, The hour has come for the Son of Humanity to be glorified.
Amen, amen, I say to you: unless the grain of wheat falling into the earth dies, it remains alone; but if it dies, it bears much fruit.
The one loving one's life/soul loses it, and the one hating one's life/soul in this world will guard it into age-long life.
If anyone serves me, let that one follow me; and where I am, there also my servant will be. If anyone serves me, the Father will honor that one.
Now my soul/life has been troubled, and what shall I say? Father, save me from this hour? But because of this I came to this hour.
Father, glorify your name. Then a voice came from heaven: I have both glorified it and will glorify again.
The crowd therefore standing and hearing said it had thundered; others said, A messenger has spoken to him.
Jesus answered and said, This voice has not happened because of me, but because of you.
Now is the judgment of this world; now the ruler of this world will be cast outside.
And I, if I am lifted up from the earth, will draw all to myself.
He was saying this signifying by what kind of death he was about to die.
The crowd answered him, We heard from the Law that the Messiah remains into the age; how do you say that the Son of Humanity must be lifted up? Who is this Son of Humanity?
Jesus therefore said to them, Yet a little time the light is among you. Walk while you have the light, so that darkness may not overtake you; the one walking in darkness does not know where one goes.
While you have the light, trust into the light, so that you may become children of light. Jesus spoke these things, and going away, he was hidden from them.
Though he had done so many signs before them, they were not trusting into him,
so that the word of Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled, which he said: Lord, who trusted our report? And the arm of the Lord, to whom was it revealed?
Because of this they were not able to trust, because again Isaiah said,
He has blinded their eyes and hardened their heart, so that they might not see with the eyes and understand with the heart and turn, and I would heal them.
Isaiah said these things because he saw his glory and spoke concerning him.
Nevertheless, even from the rulers many trusted into him, but because of the Pharisees they were not confessing, so that they would not be put out of the synagogue.
For they loved the glory of humans more than the glory of the Divine.
Jesus cried out and said, The one trusting into me trusts not into me but into the One who sent me.
And the one beholding me beholds the One who sent me.
I have come as light into the world, so that everyone trusting into me may not remain in darkness.
If anyone hears my words and does not guard them, I do not judge that one; for I did not come to judge the world, but to save/heal the world.
The one rejecting me and not receiving my words has one judging that one: the word I spoke, that will judge that one on the last day.
Because I did not speak from myself, but the Father who sent me has himself given me command, what I should say and what I should speak.
And I know that his command is age-long life. Therefore what I speak, just as the Father has said to me, so I speak.
